programme.title: Soldier, Soldier

Medical drama, starring Derek Thompson and Ian Bleasdale.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Yvonne is suspended from duty pending further investigation into the death of young thug Lee Thomas, and Leela is also accused of assaulting a suspect, leaving the relief uncertain as to what constitutes reasonable force when making an arrest. Fearful of leaving himself open to accusations of heavy-handedness or police brutality, Tony later hesitates to use force in a confrontation with local youths - and suffers a horrific beating. Meanwhile Zain Nadir agrees to do a stint in CID before beginning his next undercover job.(n)

Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept chef, and these intrepid home cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Planning the militia's next move, Jude suggests they take advantage of the fact that Callum has access to the prime minister's daughter. Is he willing to put Sephy's life in danger? Stormzy guest stars in the drama.(n)

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor goes rural property shopping in the Yorkshire Dales with a retired head teacher, who has £400,000 to invest in her leap to the country and has signed up her daughter to help her with the decision-making. Away from a high energy house hunt, Margherita discovers how one Dales company has recently been instrumental in reviving Britain's hand-made piano heritage, an echo of production glory days during the 1920s.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain.(n)

programme.desc: Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones goes in search of the legendary Ark of the Covenant, a biblical artefact of devastating power. programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: The staff of Newcastle's Theatre Royal has to get one show out and another one in, while AA mechanics come to the aid of stricken motorists and Care Call deals with emergency cases.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a semi in Surrey, a terrace in the West Midlands, and a bungalow in Ashton on Ribble, Lancashire.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo completes his rail journey through 1930s Britain, beginning in Norfolk at the headquarters of an international horse welfare organisation. In Peterborough, he visits a tidal defence barrier that helped save the city from flooding, while in Lincolnshire crosses the point at which the Mallard broke the speed record for a steam-powered locomotive in 1938. After a spell in the coastal town of Skegness, Michael's journey ends at RAF Coningsby where he sees some iconic Second World War aircraft, before witnessing a dazzling display featuring the RAF's latest combat aircraft.(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Walberswick, the former medieval port on the Suffolk coast, which is now a favourite retreat for writers, artists and day trippers. For centuries the village lurched from prosperity to poverty and in medieval times a bitter battle for supremacy was fought against the neighbouring port of Dunwich.(n)

programme.title: The Sweet Makers
programme.sub-title: The Sweet Makers: A Victorian Treat
programme.desc: This time, confectioners Paul, Cynthia, Andy and Diana use original recipes to recreate a Victorian sweet shop, and discover how the quest to satisfy our national sweet tooth transformed Britain. Guided by food historian Dr Annie Gray and social historian Emma Dabiri, the sweet-makers head to Blists Hill, Shropshire, to experience first-hand the life of confectioners in late 19th and early 20th century England. The team recreate a range of Victorian treats to stock their sweet shop, from jars of boiled sweets to their own version of fruit pastilles, and an enormous Easter egg.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: George and Judy from the Lake District take on Pauline and Krystle from London, going head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector, Richie. Paul Martin hosts.(n)

programme.title: The Dark: Nature's Nighttime World
programme.desc: Justine Evans encounters a puma at the tip of South America and films its nocturnal hunting methods for the first time. Farther south, Gordon Buchanan dives into the icy waters of the Magellan Strait to solve a mystery - scientists have heard humpback whales moving close to the shore at night, but have no idea what they could be doing. Elsewhere, George McGavin attaches a miniature tracking device to a vampire bat to discover whose blood it is feeding on.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits archive interviews with some of Britain's biggest stars of the 1960s, including Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Peter Sellers, Peter O'Toole and Julie Christie.(n)

programme.title: The Titfield Thunderbolt (1953)
programme.desc: Villagers try to save their railway branch from closure by taking it over as a public business - but the owners of the local bus company try to sabotage their plans, forcing them to resort to more underhand methods. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway, George Relph, John Gregson, Godfrey Tearle and Sid James.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to sell at auction at the Boconnoc estate in the Cornish countryside. Meanwhile, Paul Martin visits the gardens of Caerhays.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty, Jordan Stephens, Sally Lindsay and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A wolf cub's life hangs in the balance after it is attacked by the rest of the pack. Elsewhere, Ben Fogle meets his old nemesis, Rio the cockatoo.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Provence in southern France. Starting out in Marseille, they meet a Moroccan woman who runs a welcome centre where immigrants can eat together - and who makes a fish stew to die for. Travelling west, they learn about the culinary influence of the Romans who conquered Europe 2,000 years ago, try a breakfast of pastry filled with spicy octopus, and dine with a Catalan farmer and his family on a regional speciality of barbecued snails.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Shauna Froydenlund, Paul Cunningham, Leigh Ferguson and Alex Greene compete in the heats for Northern Ireland. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including an ambitious duck crumble with rhubarb starter in ode to Alice in Wonderland, and a fish dish of halibut with oysters and foraged nettles and seaweed, inspired by Enid Blyton's The Magical Faraway Tree.(n)

programme.title: The Countess and the Russian Billionaire
programme.desc: An inside look at the secret world of Sergei Pugachev and his British partner Countess Alexandra Tolstoy. A hugely rich and powerful couple, with three beautiful children, homes across the world, and unimaginable wealth, the couple appeared to have it all, but their fairy tale life is about to come crashing down around them.(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Leslie's luck appears to be on the up when his mother's boyfriend invites him to interview for a job at a posh law firm. Leslie's preparation for the interview is meticulous and he has played the whole interview out in his head, but when he arrives at the reception he realise he has forgotten one very important thing, his shoes.(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and comedy correspondents Rachel Parris, Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on everything that has - and hasn't - happened in recent news.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones consider three more money-making proposals. The ideas under deliberation are an environmentally friendly range of plastic-free water bottles made from starch, ergonomic underwear, and an armchair tray for elderly people. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams set off from Panama City across the Caribbean Sea, but they face an unplanned obstacle when a state of emergency is declared in Quito, Ecuador - the destination of their next checkpoint. The teams are forced to change route in mid-race and head into the remote Colombian desert. Rob and Jen make use of night buses to get ahead, Emon and Jamiul raise some money by teaching scuba diving, and Sam struggles with the pressure and needs a break.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The work of Liverpool's Walton Centre, the country's only specialist neurology hospital trust, as staff deal with patients suffering from the complex brain condition FND (Functional Neurological Disorder). Sixty-six-year-old Pauline has been left severely disabled by FND but there is nothing physically wrong with her body. David, a 38-year-old window cleaner, has been unable to walk more than a few steps a year. However, medical professionals are still learning about the condition - so the Walton Centre started an FND service in 2012.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A policeman saves residents from fire, a couple on a mission to save rhinos end up needing saving themselves and a woman is rescued after sailing into stormy seas.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Workers undertake major road repairs that must be completed before morning rush hour, and one of the North East's biggest bakeries toil through the night baking fresh products.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs and a couple of ploughing trophies.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s.(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: A duo from Somerset are among the contestants taking part in the antiques game show to curate the most valuable collection, with £1,000 going to the winners. Paul Martin hosts.(n)

programme.title: Passover: Why Is This Night Different?
programme.desc: Giles Coren hosts a ritual feast to mark the beginning of the Jewish holiday of Passover, an eight-day celebration widely regarded as the most cherished in the festival calendar. Guests include philosopher Alain de Botton, comedienne Olivia Lee, the Rabbis Naftali Brawer and Laura Janner-Klausner and chef Linda Dangoor.(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott serves up a family-sized slab of chocolate recipes from the BBC archives, guaranteed to please the nation's chocoholics. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' classic chocolate pudding, the Hairy Bikers 'world's best' brownies, a chocolate mousse from Raymond Blanc, James Martin's marshmallows with chocolate dipping sauce and a surprise from Nigella Lawson - chocolate pasta.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV and big screen appearances made over the years by Oscar-winning actor Alec Guinness, exploring the milestones of his life and career - including memorable roles in The Bridge on the River Kwai and Lawrence of Arabia. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Whisky Galore! (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a Hebridean island endure many months without alcohol as a result of wartime rationing. When a ship runs aground carrying thousands of cases of Scotch, the locals are determined to keep it to themselves, despite the best efforts of a puritanical English home guard officer to confiscate it. Ealing comedy, starring Basil Radford, Gordon Jackson and James Robertson Justice.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Burton Constable Hall in the East Riding of Yorkshire. Antiques expert Jonathan Pratt finds a bronze statue, and Catherine Southon enthuses about a silver bowl.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Newsreader Naga Munchetty, actor-singer Jordan Stephens, actress Sally Lindsay and funnyman David O'Doherty continue the daily test of their skills, hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Red the Amur tiger is turning out to be a tearaway teenager, so the keepers try to stop him from launching terrifying attacks on visitors' cars and running amok.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Wolds Way with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adventurer Paul Rose concludes his exploration of the 79-mile Yorkshire Wolds Way, taking to the skies to get a unique view of its stretch of chalk downland. He also receives a special invitation to a military base that has been keeping the nation safe since the start of the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels through the Borders, learning handling techniques at a school for sheepdogs and then heading to some man-made lochs in the Moorfoot Hills. In Edinburgh, he goes in search of a lost river and two long-vanished lochs in the heart of the city, before walking in the footsteps of Mary, Queen of Scots on a allegedly haunted island in Loch Leven.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland chefs cook mains and desserts celebrating children's literature, including a Hogwarts Feast which features Glenarm short horn beef, a CS Lewis-inspired Centaur's Supper Dish, based around bacon and oats, and feast fit for a Big Friendly Giant, featuring roast pork loin.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Part one of two. The facts behind the biggest public health crisis in living memory, and exploring the latest research from the frontline of the medical and scientific fightback. The world's media is awash with data, information and misinformation, but what are the facts? Among the questions asked are why this strain of coronavirus is so dangerous, what happens in our bodies when the virus attacks and how does this compare to previous pandemics? Presented by Dr Chris van Tulleken, Dr Hannah Fry, Michael Mosley and Dr Guddi Singh.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Corner Shop
programme.desc: The Arden family experience corner shop life in the 1980s and 1990s, when stores changed from primary shopping destinations to a place for last minute impulse buys and were forced to make the maximum use of their floor space to stock thousands of products. The programme examines the impact of new legislation allowing supermarkets to open on Sundays, previously the only advantage small businesses had over them. ABC's Martin Fry visits the shop and reveals how the band used to use empty industrial units as cheap rehearsal space, and there is a look at the rise of the gaming industry and a revival of a famous 1980s marketing stunt.(n)

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan returns to Minnesota in the summer to catch up with the black bears, but is upset to discover that cub Hope has been abandoned by her mother. With the young animal facing starvation, he is forced to step in and become a surrogate parent, and balances his new duties with continued study of the rest of the family.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as two teams put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection, for a chance to win £1,000.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: The Mating Game - Natural World Special
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the different methods animals use to attract a mate, revealing how inventive, loving and complex they can be. A female polar bear tries to revive a male's interest in her by turning cartwheels in front of him, while a bird of paradise tidies obsessively as he prepares a stage for his dance. While other lemurs are fighting for the right to breed, one sidles up to a female and wins favour by wafting his scent at her, and silverback gorilla seduction involves staring with a fixed grin.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Easter Story
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes, choirmaster Gareth Malone and gospel choir conductor Karen Gibson travel round the UK to uncover the stories, music and traditions that have marked the Easter Christian festivals both in the past and today. From the island of Lindisfarne to a Welsh mountain with a unique connection to Good Friday, Gareth finds out what links Easter to some of the country's most stunning historic sites. Meanwhile, Karen unravels the origins of one of the best-known Good Friday hymns and revisits some of the traditions she grew up with.(n)

programme.title: The Lavender Hill Mob (1951)
programme.desc: An unassuming bank worker masterminds a plot to steal £1million in gold bullion, then melts it down and turns it into statues of the Eiffel Tower that can easily be exported - but the plan soon goes awry, with hilarious results. Ealing comedy, starring Alec Guinness, Stanley Holloway, Sid James and Audrey Hepburn.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team visits the University of Nottingham, where antique expert Caroline Hawley is wowed by a huge Shakespearean-themed quilt and Charles Hanson comes across an 18th-century handwritten Valentine that is hotly contested at the auction. Presenter Paul Martin visits a gorge in north Nottinghamshire called Creswell Crags, where in 2003 prehistoric cave art was discovered.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer and actor Jordan Stephens, presenter and actor Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble reveal how the wild cousins of Cuban crocodiles Kang and Keo, are known for their leaping prowess. Plus, the in-built armour of hairy-nosed wombats.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Professional chefs compete for a place at a banquet celebrating children's literature. Here, the two highest-scoring chefs from Northern Ireland cook their six-course menus again for Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver, who are joined by guest Cerrie Burnell, who writes children's books with an emphasis on diversity and inclusivity. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the days get longer and warmer, the Gardeners' World team returns to guide enthusiasts through seasonal highlights from across the country visiting stunning gardens, meeting the gardeners and finding out their secrets of success. This week, Adam Frost heads into his garden to offer inspiration for viewers' own patches along with all the essential gardening jobs for the coming weekend.(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Istanbul
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles, Edwina Currie, Fatima Whitbread, Mim Shaikh, Amar Latif, Dom Joly and Pauline McLynn still have 650km to travel to reach Istanbul. The last leg of their route takes them through more Bulgarian forest in the Rhodope Mountains, involving a tricky 1,500m climb. Near the summit, the incline increases, though the breath-taking view proves worth it. In Batak, they visit the small orthodox church of St Nedelya where around 5,000 people were massacred by Ottoman militia in the 19th century. Eventually, they arrive in Turkey, heading for the magnificent Suleymaniye Mosque in Istanbul.(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Britain's most uncompromising topical satire tackles all the troubling news from a world that's put its faith in Boris Johnson and Donald Trump. Nish Kumar leads an array of quarantined correspondents, including Rachel Parris, Geoff Norcott and Ellie Taylor.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan, Matt Lucas and Liza Tarbuck join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Odds and Ends'. During the course of the show, the panellists learn how to tell a schnauzerorgan from a paddywhack. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct.(n)

programme.title: The Bling Ring (2013)
programme.desc: Five teenagers united by an obsession with celebrity steal from the houses of the rich and famous stars they idolise, using the internet to follow their targets' movements. When the police finally catch up with them, the arrest brings a taste of the fame they have craved. Fact-based drama, starring Emma Watson and Israel Broussard.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: The production of the traditional garment, following the painstaking process of the silk being bound, hand-dyed, woven and finally turned into a kimono by a seamstress.(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy witnesses the impact of Anne Boleyn on Henry VIII's affections, uncovering private moments that were reported by eyewitnesses or recorded in historical documents. The key moments during their marriage are recreated, as Anne falls from favour in court and is eventually executed, despite protestations of innocence regarding the rumours of her infidelity. As soon as Anne is dead, Henry marries Jane Seymour, who fulfils her most important role as Queen - giving birth to the healthy son that Henry has craved for so long.(n)

programme.title: Secret Universe: The Hidden Life of the Cell
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of what happens to a single human cell as it comes under attack from a virus, demonstrating how it defends itself from the hostile organism. The film uses CGI to show the membrane that acts as a security system, the remarkable turbines that power the cellular world, the nucleus housing DNA and the construction of thousands of different proteins all with unique tasks. David Tennant narrates.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Predicting what may happen when the next flu pandemic occurs, stemming from a virus originating in the stomach of a dead duck. The Government's Influenza Contingency Plan highlights the possibility of the strain wiping out up to 750,000 people in Britain, regardless of health, and overwhelming hospitals and emergency services in the process.(n)

programme.title: Horse People with Alexandra Tolstoy
programme.desc: The globe-trotting adventurer draws her quest to a close in the western American state of Montana, where the horse is still an important part of the culture. She meets modern-day cowboys at a traditional ranch, where she witnesses horse-whispering and takes part in a barrel race. Later, she joins Native American herders as they head to round up wild buffalo in the mountains.(n)

programme.title: The Banker's Guide to the Art Market
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the closed doors of London's art market, revealing how the newly rich of the financial world has pushed prices to unprecedented heights.(n)

programme.title: Rhyme and Reason: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: A compilation of short films, offering a snapshot of a new generation of artists who have been inspired by poetry and the spoken word.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Predicting what may happen when the next flu pandemic occurs, stemming from a virus originating in the stomach of a dead duck. The Government's Influenza Contingency Plan highlights the possibility of the strain wiping out up to 750,000 people in Britain, regardless of health, and overwhelming hospitals and emergency services in the process.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham learns how foxes have been able to adapt and thrive in a world with an ever-increasing human population. New research reveals how the animals' split pupils enable them to hunt in the bright desert day, and the presenter discusses a theory that the creatures use the Earth's magnetic field to determine the location of their prey.(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin examines the toothbrush, an item so ubiquitous that most people do not give it a moment's thought - and yet 200 million of them are thrown away each year. Zoe sets out to create a sustainable solution, heading to a low-impact factory in Sweden where handles are made from sugarcane bio-based plastic and exploring bespoke production techniques employing 3D laser sintering.(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.sub-title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema: The Rom-Com
programme.desc: The critic examines techniques and conventions behind classic movie genres. Here he focuses on one of the most popular - romantic comedies, from early offerings such as Bringing Up Baby to more recent hits like The Big Sick and La La Land.(n)

programme.title: Dawn French Live: 30 Million Minutes
programme.desc: A recording of the celebrated comedian's critically acclaimed one-woman show, filmed during its final West End run in 2016. In 30 Million Minutes, Dawn takes her audience on a journey through her life's experiences, detailing irksome tribulations, crucial lessons that she has learned along the way, and things 'she knows to be true'. With a sharp eye for comic detail and an ear for the absurdities of life, Dawn draws on her personal and professional personas to offer an alternative view to modern living.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Arena: Loretta Lynn - Still a Mountain Girl
programme.desc: The veteran country music singer and songwriter looks back at her life from being born a coal miner's daughter in Kentucky, to selling more than 45 million albums worldwide and to receiving the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Barack Obama in 2013. Contributors include Willie Nelson, Sheryl Crow, Garth Brooks, Jack White and Sissy Spacek.(n)

programme.title: Smile! The Nation's Family Album
programme.desc: A revealing portrait that documents the changes in digital technology and how we capture images in the modern day, looking back across generations and the way that families collected their sentimental snapshots and shared experiences, offering an intimate portrait of Britain's post-war social history.(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin examines the toothbrush, an item so ubiquitous that most people do not give it a moment's thought - and yet 200 million of them are thrown away each year. Zoe sets out to create a sustainable solution, heading to a low-impact factory in Sweden where handles are made from sugarcane bio-based plastic and exploring bespoke production techniques employing 3D laser sintering.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first shown on June 29, featuring performances by Holly Johnson, Queen, Donna Allen, Guns N' Roses, London Boys and Double Trouble with Rebel MC.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on July 6, featuring performances by Sonia, Monie Love, Gladys Knight, Karyn White, Chaka Khan, Pet Shop Boys and the Beautiful South.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s
programme.desc: Archive performances by prominent soul artists and groups of the decade, including Aretha Franklin, Billy Preston, the Tams, Curtis Mayfield, Bill Withers, the Stylistics, Gil Scott-Heron and the Jacksons.(n)

programme.title: Kenny Rogers: Cards on the Table
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling Rogers' career and the golden era of country music he helped usher in, reflecting on how he rose to become a superstar selling over 120 million albums worldwide. Making use of a candid interview with the performer who died recently, the film also looks at his success in other fields, including tennis and photography. Contributors include singer, songwriter and producer Kim Carnes, actor and musician Mickey Jones and long-time friend Lionel Richie.(n)

programme.title: David Cassidy: The Last Session
programme.desc: Candid biography exploring the life of the '70s pop idol and TV star, including never-before-heard audio interviews with David from 1976 and a raw and poignantly filmed final recording session shortly before his death in November 2017. Cassidy's role as singer Keith Partridge in US sitcom The Partridge Family brought fame and fortune and made him a global teen sensation. This documentary tells the inside story of his explosive rise to fame, his conflicted relationship with his father and his own celebrity and the legacy he has left behind. Contributors include musician Kim Carnes.(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Five talented amateurs compete to win a place in the finals. Each must cook an outstanding dish to win over four of the country's most feared restaurant critics - Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept chef, and these intrepid home cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so with the pressure firmly on it promises to be a tense and thrilling affair. At the end, judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace will no doubt face a difficult decision.(n)

programme.desc: Sephy decides she does not want to accept her place at university in Aprica and leaves home with Jasmine - but she is harbouring a huge secret. Meanwhile, Callum and Jude, both working with the Liberation Militia, stage an attack on the offices of the Ohene Standard newspaper, leaving the editor Temitope Kolawale brutally beaten. Planning the militia's next move, Jude suggests they take advantage of the fact that Callum has access to the prime minister's daughter. Is he willing to put Sephy's life in danger? Stormzy guest stars in the drama.(n)

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden.(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the contestants assisting each of them. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor goes rural property shopping in the Yorkshire Dales with a retired head teacher, who has £400,000 to invest in her leap to the country and has signed up her daughter to help her with the decision-making. Away from a high energy house hunt, Margherita discovers how one Dales company has recently been instrumental in reviving Britain's hand-made piano heritage, an echo of production glory days during the 1920s.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain.(n)

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

Next, the contestants have to create a dish inspired by someone they love or admire as they battle for their place in The Finals.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Long Can it Last? Tonight
programme.desc: Just a couple of months ago the economy was riding high with employment at record levels. But that was before the coronavirus epidemic tightened its grip on Britain, ripping up the way of life. In the past 14 days half a million people have signed on for Universal Credit. With the Government projected to spend many tens of billions in a bid to keep the economy, and jobs, afloat, Sonali Shah investigates how long the lockdown can last.(n)

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure

programme.desc: Comedian, actor and travel enthusiast Griff Rhys Jones journeys from Brisbane to Cairns aboard the Spirit of Queensland railway - and during his various stops, he comes across all manner of things that seem determined to kill him, from jellyfish to dingoes to whitewater rapids and more.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test
programme.desc: Cameras capture more would-be drivers as they sit their driving tests. Fifty-six-year-old Julie is desperate to pass on her third attempt, even though she has not driven in more than 24 years, while make-up artist Bradley serenades his driving examiner. In Hull, fiercely competitive father and daughter duo Rich and Yalana have their tests on the same day. Who will triumph? Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay aims to show his companions that US food lives up to its reputation of being bigger and better as they travel from Nevada to Los Angeles. They begin in style with a helicopter ride about the Grand Canyon - although Gino is too nervous to appreciate the spectacle below him. Next up is a trout fishing trip contest in the Colorado River, followed by a burger in Las Vegas with a hefty price tag of $777 and a visit to a ranch where they have to round up herds of cattle on horseback. Finally, they hit Los Angeles, where they have a barbecue battle.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Long Can it Last? Tonight
programme.desc: Just a couple of months ago the economy was riding high with employment at record levels. But that was before the coronavirus epidemic tightened its grip on Britain, ripping up the way of life. In the past 14 days half a million people have signed on for Universal Credit. programme.title: Secrets of the Driving Test

In Hull, fiercely competitive father and daughter duo Rich and Yalana have their tests on the same day. Who will triumph? Narrated by Alan Carr.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay aims to show his companions that US food lives up to its reputation of being bigger and better as they travel from Nevada to Los Angeles. They begin in style with a helicopter ride about the Grand Canyon - although Gino is too nervous to appreciate the spectacle below him. Next up is a trout fishing trip contest in the Colorado River, followed by a burger in Las Vegas with a hefty price tag of $777 and a visit to a ranch where they have to round up herds of cattle on horseback. Finally, they hit Los Angeles, where they have a barbecue battle.(n)

programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Richard Osman, Gregg Wallace and Penny Lancaster to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.sub-title: Funniest Ever You've Been Framed! Gold
programme.desc: Harry Hill introduces another rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities and home-video howlers, featuring practical jokers, naughty animals, musical mishaps and holiday clips.(n)

programme.title: Alvin and the Chipmunks: Chipwrecked (2011)
programme.desc: The singing rodents cause mayhem while performing on a tropical cruise, before they find themselves stranded on a desert island. As they search for a way to get back to civilisation, it becomes clear that they are not alone. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Lee and Jenny Slate, with the voices of Justin Long, Matthew Gray Gubler and Jesse McCartney.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.desc: Harry Hill opens his diary to look at a typical year in the life of a You've Been Framed! star, taking in all the major events in the calendar, from Pancake Day disasters to horrific Halloweens.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedy from Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos. Featuring a toddler who destroys Stonehenge, babysitting tips from a cross-dressing dad, the world's first heavy-petting zoo and a seagull that loves electronic dance music.(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Highlights from the game show in which Stephen Mulhern sets members of the public unusual challenges in locations around the UK.(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Nottingham.(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: A special edition of the game show in which host Stephen Mulhern invites comedy pairings to take place. Inside No 9 stars Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, Danny John-Jules and Robert Llewellyn of Red Dwarf fame and Nina Wadia and Lynda Baron, who co-star in Still Open All Hours, are the famous duos taking part. As usual they try to guess the popular phrases in the hope of scoring enough points to win a cash prize for their charity of choice - and making it through to the Super Catchphrase to play for £50,000.(n)

programme.title: Antz (1998)
programme.desc: A neurotic worker ant passes himself off as a soldier to impress a princess. However, he provokes a jealously ambitious general with an accidental act of heroism - so he and his beloved decide to flee the brutal colony and go in search of the promised land of Insectopia. Animated comedy, with the voices of Woody Allen, Sharon Stone, Gene Hackman and Sylvester Stallone.(n)

Part-animated comedy, starring James Marsden, with the voice of Russell Brand.(n)

programme.desc: Steve panics when the cheque from Costellos bounces. Deirdre breaks down when tests show she isn't a suitable donor for Tracy. Alf is furious to find Audrey flirting with Brian.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera considers taking Tommy on holiday. Des feels down on his 30th birthday. Denise does her best to avoid Ken. Steve asks Vicky to invest more money in him.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry teases Eric about going out with a policewoman. The Dingles receive a frosty reception in the Woolpack. Tina visits the scene of Luke's death.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty spots a mystery man watching Eric and Barbara together. Rachel is worried that Chris is avoiding spending time with the baby. Kim and Dave make plans to meet up in Leeds.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When teenager Vivienne Middleton is reported missing Joe suspects her disappearance has something to do with her American boyfriend Bobby, and sets out to locate him. Meanwhile, David and Peggy's latest exploit, to use Greengrass's old metal detector to find artefacts about the village, puts them under suspicion when priceless bronze statues are stolen from local estates. Guest starring Adrian Lukis and Tessa Peake-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim sustains an injury, leaving Roger with the responsibility of organising the team teas, and Alex's insecure girlfriend puts in an appearance. Cricketing comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn and Robert Daws.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's pre-match strategy has a dramatic impact in the team's hotel room on the night before the Cromer game. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and Carol join forces to unravel the mystery of a girl resident at Throckton Grange, when she collapses with exhaustion and expresses a fear of being taken away. Meanwhile, Geoff suffers from embarrassing bouts of sneezing when he tries to speak to Lucy who is temporarily working at the village shop, and David is surprised to be offered an unusual post at the local newspaper. Simon Shepherd and Victor McGuire guest star.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The inquest into Luke's death arrives at a verdict, leading to a clash between Tina and Jessica. Frank unexpectedly gatecrashes Kim and Dave's tryst at a hotel in Leeds.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is annoyed when Steve spends more time with Vicky. Deirdre is thrilled when Ken decides to have tests to see if he can be a donor. Vicky signs up for a business course.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken lays down his access requests to Denise. Samir tells Deirdre and Ken he will see if he is a suitable donor for Tracy. Jack agrees to let Jamie look after his pigeons.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and Carol join forces to unravel the mystery of a girl resident at Throckton Grange, when she collapses with exhaustion and expresses a fear of being taken away. Meanwhile, Geoff suffers from embarrassing bouts of sneezing when he tries to speak to Lucy who is temporarily working at the village shop, and David is surprised to be offered an unusual post at the local newspaper. Simon Shepherd and Victor McGuire guest star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Italian Nobleman
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Leonard gets caught up in an armed robbery at the village post office, and manages to identify the masked culprit. Geordie takes no chances when going to arrest the criminal, who has a record of similarly violent crimes - but is surprised to discover that the suspect has been murdered. Meanwhile, a second robbery occurs, carried out by a man and a woman who have adopted the dead robber's trademark of disguising himself with a gas mask. Back at the Vicarage, the atmosphere is tense between Sidney and Amanda.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The day of the Cromer game dawns and the team are determined to beat Fatty Woollacott's side at all costs - but a mysterious incident in Dorking threatens to put a spanner in the works. Comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park looks ahead to a new season, Roger tries to make amends with Miriam before whipping the team into shape. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe wants to carry on as normal after the attack, but Emma wants her to report it. Sam gets fed up of being bossed around by Eric. Frank confronts Ken Adlington.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is worried that Frank has found out about her and Dave. Sam enlists Butch to help with Eric's house clearance. Zoe makes a statement to the police about the attack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona panics over keeping the salon going. Samir receives good news from the doctors about becoming a donor. Jon tells Ken that Denise has left and taken Daniel with her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre, Samir and Tracy struggle for space in Ken's flat. Martin finds out the results of his nursing exams. Fiona calls in her friend Maxine to help out at the salon.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A factory worker dies in what appears to be a drunken accident, but Geordie suspects foul play. However, secret forces are determined to shut down his investigation. Mrs Maguire is confronted by a face from the past, while Sidney wrestles with his conscience, as his feelings for Amanda clash with his sense of duty - leading to accusations of hypocrisy from Leonard.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Italian Nobleman
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The day of the Cromer game dawns and the team are determined to beat Fatty Woollacott's side at all costs - but a mysterious incident in Dorking threatens to put a spanner in the works. Comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Temperamental tenant Spooner, a professional wrestler, is confined to his room with a broken leg, and makes life hell for Alan and Philip by playing his radio full-blast at all hours - so naturally they challenge Rigsby to confront him. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Doctor (1967)
programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: On the Buses (1971)
programme.desc: Wily bus driver Stan Butler thinks a new company policy that allows women behind the wheel will rob him of his precious overtime money. Enlisting the aid of conductor Jack, he does everything he can to sabotage the plan. Comedy, based on the TV series, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant, Stephen Lewis, Doris Hare, Peter Madden and Anna Karen.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Behind (1975)
programme.desc: Professors Ronald Crump and Anna Vooshka embark on an archaeological dig, but find their efforts disrupted by the saucy shenanigans of a motley bunch of holiday-makers in the neighbouring caravan park. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Elke Sommer, Joan Sims, Windsor Davies, Kenneth Connor and Bernard Bresslaw.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Investigations into the identity of the victim of a car crash uncovers connections to violent criminals, and leads to worries about his intended business in Aidensfield. Not realising their true value, Peggy tries to offload some goats when she finds them too difficult to milk. Oscar takes on the surveillance of a woman whose husband suspects she is having an affair, leaving Dawn and Bernie in charge of the bar. Mark Moraghan and Adam Christopher guest star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: A nasty stomach bug sweeps through Portwenn and the doctor suspects the local water supply is to blame, taking to the airwaves in a bid to warn everyone. Meanwhile, Al remains determined to leave the plumbing business and attend college, prompting an argument with Bert. Medical comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Joe Absolom and Caroline Catz.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa tries to help one of her pupils, a bright 10-year-old who finds it hard to fit in and responds to stress by running away from home. Mark seeks advice on how to remedy his lack of success with the opposite sex, while Martin encounters a delusional park ranger with an unusual imaginary friend - a giant squirrel called Anthony. Medical comedy drama series, starring Martin Clunes.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney tries to put his life in Grantchester behind him by going in search of the man who stole Mrs Maguire's life savings, and ends up finding a new home in a travellers' community. However, he falls under suspicions when a murder rocks the camp. Geordie tracks his friend down and together they set out to solve the mystery - and hopefully give Sidney a sense of purpose in his life.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney has lost his faith in the church, so decides to resign so he can marry Amanda. His decision is a crushing blow to Mrs Maguire, while Leonard fears the arrival of a less tolerant vicar. Sidney soon finds the community needs him more than ever when a boy is abducted, while Geordie's behaviour grows more and more unstable as he wrestles with family problems. As the divided vicar grows closer to the truth about the missing child, he is forced to choose between love, faith, and loyalty to his troubled friend.(n)

programme.desc: Fiona is annoyed when Steve spends more time with Vicky. Deirdre is thrilled when Ken decides to have tests to see if he can be a donor. Vicky signs up for a business course.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken lays down his access requests to Denise. Samir tells Deirdre and Ken he will see if he is a suitable donor for Tracy. Jack agrees to let Jamie look after his pigeons.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and Carol join forces to unravel the mystery of a girl resident at Throckton Grange, when she collapses with exhaustion and expresses a fear of being taken away. Meanwhile, Geoff suffers from embarrassing bouts of sneezing when he tries to speak to Lucy who is temporarily working at the village shop, and David is surprised to be offered an unusual post at the local newspaper. Simon Shepherd and Victor McGuire guest star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Italian Nobleman
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Leonard gets caught up in an armed robbery at the village post office, and manages to identify the masked culprit. Geordie takes no chances when going to arrest the criminal, who has a record of similarly violent crimes - but is surprised to discover that the suspect has been murdered. Meanwhile, a second robbery occurs, carried out by a man and a woman who have adopted the dead robber's trademark of disguising himself with a gas mask. Back at the Vicarage, the atmosphere is tense between Sidney and Amanda.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's pre-match strategy has a dramatic impact in the team's hotel room on the night before the Cromer game. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: The day of the Cromer game dawns and the team are determined to beat Fatty Woollacott's side at all costs - but a mysterious incident in Dorking threatens to put a spanner in the works. Comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park looks ahead to a new season, Roger tries to make amends with Miriam before whipping the team into shape. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe wants to carry on as normal after the attack, but Emma wants her to report it. Sam gets fed up of being bossed around by Eric. Frank confronts Ken Adlington.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is worried that Frank has found out about her and Dave. Sam enlists Butch to help with Eric's house clearance. Zoe makes a statement to the police about the attack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona panics over keeping the salon going. Samir receives good news from the doctors about becoming a donor. Jon tells Ken that Denise has left and taken Daniel with her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre, Samir and Tracy struggle for space in Ken's flat. Martin finds out the results of his nursing exams. Fiona calls in her friend Maxine to help out at the salon.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The peace of the sleepy hamlet of Little Worthy is disturbed when a man's body is found tied down in a miniature village - just like the hero in classic tale Gulliver's Travels. As the investigation begins, Barnaby has no shortage of suspects, including the victim's recently jilted partner - a teacher whose classroom is full of paintings of the story - his teenage lover, a caretaker and a hotel owner. But the case takes a twist when the killer strikes again amid the chaos of the annual fancy-dress boat race. Detective drama, guest starring Jesse Birdsall and Olivia Colman, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phil's niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A case from 13 years earlier returns to haunt Lewis when the evidence comes under review. Graham Lawrie was incarcerated in a secure psychiatric hospital after being found guilty of the murders of three police officers, but now the findings of the forensics lab have been called into question and an appeal has been launched. As Lawrie remains locked up ahead of his day in court, PC Mark Travis is lured to an isolated location with a hoax call and falls victim to a killer who uses the same weapon as that used in the original murders.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A factory worker dies in what appears to be a drunken accident, but Geordie suspects foul play. However, secret forces are determined to shut down his investigation. Mrs Maguire is confronted by a face from the past, while Sidney wrestles with his conscience, as his feelings for Amanda clash with his sense of duty - leading to accusations of hypocrisy from Leonard.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Italian Nobleman
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)
